items.title: How I use Claude Code Agent Teams (Meta Staff Engineer Tips)
items.description: Most people use Claude Code as a single agent. But when you need to coordinate multiple tasks across different domains, agent ...
extracted_patterns: One leader (orchestrator) + many teammates model â€” create composition for each workflow (e.g., architecture+backend+frontend, research+editing+writing)
Task list as coordination mechanism â€” use plan mode to create discrete tasks, then spawn agents to work on them in parallel
Message-based context sharing â€” agents communicate via messages, not full context duplication; send explicit context if agents lack information
Colored text identification â€” spawned sub-agents display with colored text in Claude Code UI for visual distinction
Dynamic teammate direction â€” can message any teammate directly, not just the orchestrator; leader can control/redirect any sub-agent
Context isolation principle â€” unlike sub-agents (which only see seeded initial context), agent teams share a common task list and coordinate via messages
Task batching for visibility â€” create 5 tasks at a time to get clear picture of parallel work; avoid overwhelming the orchestrator
Context insufficiency handling â€” if sub-agent lacks context, either have orchestrator send additional context or embed more detail in task definitions
Shared task coordination â€” agents work on common task set and communicate progress/blockers to each other and orchestrator
Use agent teams over raw sub-agents for: shared state, inter-agent coordination, and complex workflows requiring multiple specialized roles

items.title: Build Powerful AI Agents: An Introduction to the Claude Agent SDK by Anthropic
items.description: Ready to build truly powerful and reliable AI agents? Discover the Claude Agent SDK, the official developer toolkit from Anthropic.

items.title: The greatest Claude Code workflow ever (10x your speed)
items.description: This is the best Claude Code workflow ever. Here's how to use Ghostty and the Claude Code CLI to get maximum productivity.
extracted_patterns: Use Ghosty terminal instead of VS Code/Cursor for Claude Codeâ€”eliminates performance degradation from multiple IDE instances
Activate plan mode immediately (Shift+Tab twice) when starting Claude Code sessions
Split Ghosty terminals to run servers and Claude Code side-by-side simultaneously
Use Ghosty tabs for efficient multitasking across multiple Claude Code projects without memory overhead
Monitor that CLI version receives feature updates before extension/desktop app versions
Run hundreds of concurrent Claude Code terminal instances without system slowdown
Customize Ghosty with themes/colorways for optimized developer experience
Install Claude Code CLI via command line, sign up for Claude subscription
Access Claude Code by typing 'claude' in Ghosty terminal

items.title: 5 Useful Tips For Building with Claude Code (2025)
items.description: Anthropic is leading the AI Industry with models - like Sonnet and Opus - and developer tools - like Claude Code. In this video, Jon ...
extracted_patterns: Plan-driven development: Request explicit detailed plans with phases, review before committing, zoom into one phase at a time with commit gates between phases
Confidence-gated clarification: Instruct Claude to ask clarifying questions when <90% confident to prevent over-engineering and unnecessary changes
Multi-option exploration: Ask Claude to research and provide 2-3 different solution approaches before committing to implementation
Progressive commitment: Strict cycle of Build â†’ Review â†’ Commit before advancing to next phase (prevents accumulating unfixed problems)
Early exit strategy: Stop execution immediately when Claude diverges, provide targeted clarification, resume (saves tokens and reduces churn)
Context feeding for debugging: Supply images, error messages, and structured data as inline context to improve Claude's analysis

items.title: Claude Code in Slack
items.description: Delegate tasks to Claude Code directly from Slack, making it easy to move context from Slack conversations to coding sessions.
